title: RHCSA 10 Lab 02: IPv4 Networking
description: Configure persistent IPv4 networking with NetworkManager

tasks: Configure the active client connection with IPv4 address 192.168.122.45/24.
Ensure the connection uses manual IPv4 configuration and autoconnects.
hints: Use nmcli connection show --active to find the connection.
Modify the connection profile, then bring it up.
checks: nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show 'System eth1' | grep -qx '192.168.122.45/24'
nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show 'System eth1' | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show 'System eth1' | grep -qx '192.168.122.3'
nmcli -g ipv4.method,connection.autoconnect connection show 'System eth1' | grep -qx manual && nmcli -g connection.autoconnect connection show 'System eth1' | grep -qx yes
solution_outline: Make the requested configuration persistent and verify it before finishing.
task_titles: Configure the active client connection with IPv4 address 192.168.122.45/
Ensure the connection uses manual IPv4 configuration and autoconnects
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify 'System eth1' ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3
nmcli connection modify 'System eth1' ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
